sections.content:
Wait - I clicked MOUSE(row=21, col=39) which is the same as clicking the N-shape node 13 at [20-22, 38-40]. This is the same as clicking the N-shape itself, not the charcoal box. The charcoal box is node 10 with boundary [19-23, 37-41], so I should click on a part of the box that's NOT the N-shape inside it.
The N-shape (node 13) has boundary [[20, 38], [20, 40], [20, 39], [21, 38], [22, 39], [22, 40], [22, 38]]. So the N-shape occupies cells at rows 20-22, cols 38-40.
The charcoal box (node 10) has boundary [[19, 37], [19, 41], [23, 41], [23, 37]]. So the box occupies rows 19-23, cols 37-41.
To click on the charcoal box but NOT the N-shape, I need to click on the border of the box. The box border would be at row 19, row 23, col 37, or col 41.
Let me try clicking on row 19, col 39 (top border of the box, above the N-shape).
Actually wait, let me reconsider the whole problem. Maybe I'm overcomplicating this. Let me look at what the initial state was and what changes I've made.
The initial state had:
- Green source: N-shape (14px)
- Yellow source: Y-shape (23px)
- Two N-shapes in destination (7px each)
- Two Y-shapes in destination (7px each)
Current state:
- Green source: N-shape (5px)
- Yellow source: Y-shape (14px)
- Two N-shapes in destination (7px each)
- Two Y-shapes in destination (7px each)
The destination shapes haven't changed - only the sources have been toggled. So clicking destination shapes only affects sources.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is NOT to empty the sources but to do something else entirely. Let me look at the red cross patterns more carefully. They're at:
- Top right: [9-11, 51-53] - a cross pattern
- Bottom left: [51-53, 9-11] - a cross pattern
These look like they could be targets or indicators. Let me try clicking on them.
Actually, let me first try clicking on the charcoal box border to see if that does something different.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking [21,39] hit the N-shape inside the box, not the box itself.
# Need to click on the charcoal box border (row 19 or 23, col 37 or 41).
# Let me try clicking the top border of box 10: row 19, col 39

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 39}])
print("Clicked charcoal box border (19, 39):")
print("Result:", last_action_result)

if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    before = last_transition.before_frame
    after = last_transition.after_frame
    before_seg = before.segmentation
    after_seg = after.segmentation
    
    # Compare N and Y pixel counts
    before_n = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
    after_n = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
    before_y = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
    after_y = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
    
    print(f"N before: {before_n}, after: {after_n}")
    print(f"Y before: {before_y}, after: {after_y}")
    
    # Check for new/changed nodes
    before_ids = {n['id'] for n in before_seg['nodes']}
    after_ids = {n['id'] for n in after_seg['nodes']}
    if before_ids != after_ids:
        print(f"Node IDs changed: added={after_ids-before_ids}, removed={before_ids-after_ids}")

# If no gameplay change, try clicking red cross pattern
if not last_action_result.get('board_changed', False) or last_action_result.get('board_changed') and not any(before_n.get(k,0) != after_n.get(k,0) for k in set(before_n)|set(after_n)):
    print("\nNo gameplay change from box border click. Trying red cross...")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 52}])
    print("Clicked red cross (10, 52):")
    print("Result:", last_action_result)
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Destinations unchanged. Need to find win condition.", "goal_model": "Unknown - trying charcoal box borders and red crosses", "recent_findings": ["Clicking destination shapes toggles sources", "Clicking source frame: no effect"], "open_questions": ["What does clicking charcoal box border do?", "What does clicking red cross do?"], "current_plan": "Try charcoal box border, then red cross if no effect", "strategy_log": ["Toggling sources: no completion", "Clicking source frame: no effect"], "failed_probes": ["red dot click", "source frame click", "all toggle combinations"]}}
</parameter>
